qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Authenticated SSRF via workflow import-from-url endpoint allows arbitrary internal HTTP requests
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets GET /rest/workflows/from-url forwarding an attacker-supplied URL into axios.get without SSRF filtering, while the advisory concerns the Merge node SQL query mode allowing authenticated workflow authors to execute arbitrary code and write files on the n8n server.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The only qualifying finding is an SSRF in the workflow import-from-url endpoint; the advisory is remote code execution and arbitrary file write through the Merge node's SQL query mode.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-wxx7-mcgf-j869 describes authenticated users with workflow create/modify permissions leveraging the Merge node SQL query mode to execute arbitrary code and write files.

ssrf-workflow-import-from-url/vulnerability_report.json identifies packages/cli/src/workflows/workflows.controller.ts:310 and packages/@n8n/api-types/src/dto/workflows/import-workflow-from-url.dto.ts:5 as an SSRF path through axios.get(query.url).
.judge/input_manifest.json lists ssrf-workflow-import-from-url as the only real final finding with status=qualifying_vuln.
